prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "死虫式" (dead bug) exercise, side profile view, the body lying on its back on the ground horizontally, viewed from the right side. Person posture: Athletic person mid-rep of a dead bug exercise — supine on the floor with lower back pressed flat into the ground (no lumbar arch). The DIAGONAL CONTRALATERAL EXTENSION pattern is showing: the RIGHT ARM is FULLY EXTENDED overhead toward the back of the head almost touching the ground beyond the head, and SIMULTANEOUSLY the LEFT LEG is FULLY EXTENDED FORWARD parallel to the floor just a few centimeters above the ground (knee straight, toes pointed forward). The OPPOSITE pair is held TUCKED: the LEFT ARM is bent with the hand pointing toward the ceiling, and the RIGHT KNEE is bent at 90 degrees with the right thigh perpendicular to the floor (right hip and knee both at 90 degrees, right shin parallel to floor). So at any moment, ONE arm is extended overhead and the OPPOSITE leg is extended forward, while the other arm and leg stay tucked. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: this is a DIAGONAL extension drill — RIGHT ARM down + LEFT LEG forward extended; LEFT ARM up + RIGHT KNEE tucked.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: deep core (transverse abdominis and rectus abdominis) for anti-extension. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors of the bent leg and shoulder stabilizers. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹横肌" pointing to the transverse abdominis (deep core); "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "髂腰肌 / 肩稳定肌" pointing to the hip flexors and shoulder stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
